key_highlights: S&P 500 hits fresh record on strong April jobs report (115K jobs added) and Iran ceasefire hopes, heading for 6th straight weekly gain.
Iran war remains the dominant macro risk, with oil buffer draining at unprecedented pace, $5 gasoline risk, and DOJ probing suspicious oil trades.
Apple and Intel reach preliminary chip-making agreement, signaling strategic realignment in semiconductor supply chain.
Goldman Sachs flags troubling big tech trend, while Apollo CEO warns of 35% chance of major market shock from AI-driven upheaval.
Jim Cramer bullish on multiple stocks (Arista, Meta, IBM, Taiwan Semi, Palantir, Goldman Sachs) but cautious on software sector and gold.
Quantinuum (Honeywell-backed) files for US IPO, BlackRock readies tokenized money-market funds, Safepoint (Florida/Louisiana insurer) also files for IPO.
ECB's Lagarde torn between acting too early or too late; Goldman sees Fed cuts delayed to December, March on inflation.
New Zealand brain drain to Australia accelerates; Kenya stalls $1B Microsoft-G42 data centre plan due to power constraints.
China's energy imports plunge as Hormuz shipments choked; Iran war drives Africa's fertilizer crisis.
Crypto industry holds lap-dance party in bear market; Bitcoin and Ethereum hold steady after strong jobs report.

sectors.trend: Mixed but with strong pockets: Apple-Intel chip deal signals reshoring and strategic partnerships; Nvidia heading for record on AI expansion; Taiwan Semi overwhelmed with demand. However, Goldman Sachs warns of troubling big tech trend, and software sector is under pressure (Cramer notes market hates software). AI-linked job cuts continue (Cloudflare cuts 1,100+ jobs).
sectors.implications: Semiconductor and AI infrastructure remain strong, but software and some hardware names face headwinds. Investors should favor companies with direct AI exposure and strong execution.

sectors.trend: Iran war is draining global oil buffer at unprecedented pace; risk of $5 gasoline is real. China's energy imports plunge due to Hormuz disruption. DOJ probing suspicious oil trades. Gold and silver headed for weekly gains, but Cramer is not bullish on gold.
sectors.implications: Energy prices remain elevated and volatile. Oil & gas stocks may benefit in short term, but geopolitical risk is extreme. Fertilizer crisis in Africa adds to food inflation concerns.

sectors.trend: Goldman Sachs sees Fed cuts delayed to December/March; Cramer bullish on Goldman Sachs. BlackRock launching tokenized money-market funds. CION and Chimera Investment reported earnings. Safepoint (insurer) files for IPO.
sectors.implications: Higher-for-longer rates benefit banks but pressure rate-sensitive sectors. Tokenization trend is growing. Insurance sector faces Florida/Louisiana hurricane risk.

sectors.trend: Spirit Airlines folded, leading to zero flights at some airports; viral plan to revive Spirit. Frontier Airlines had runway incident. Air India cutting costs and flights due to war. Commuters paying $1,600/month for gas.
sectors.implications: Airlines face severe headwinds from high fuel costs and geopolitical disruption. Consolidation likely. Avoid airline stocks unless deep value play.

sectors.trend: Thermo Fisher and Becton Dickinson reported strong execution; Cramer recommends buying Thermo Fisher. Trump planning to fire FDA Commissioner. Health officials downplay hantavirus pandemic risk.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains defensive with selective opportunities. Regulatory uncertainty under Trump administration could create volatility.

sectors.trend: Warren Buffett sends blunt message on mortgages; mortgage rates moving back up (30-year, 15-year). Some commuters reconsidering where to live due to gas costs.
sectors.implications: Higher rates continue to pressure housing affordability. Real estate investment trusts (REITs) may face headwinds. Mortgage originators could struggle.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin and Ethereum holding steady after strong jobs report. Crypto industry throws lap-dance party in bear market. BlackRock launching tokenized money-market funds. Trump Media reports $2.2B in assets with crypto holdings.
sectors.implications: Crypto markets are resilient but still in bearish sentiment. Institutional adoption via tokenization is a positive long-term trend. Regulatory clarity remains mixed.

sectors.trend: Kenya stalls $1B Microsoft-G42 data centre plan due to power constraints. Nigerian stock market bounces back with N3.24tn gains. Pakistan receives $1.32B from IMF. China summit stays on track despite Iran concerns.
sectors.implications: Emerging markets are mixed: Africa faces infrastructure and power challenges, but Nigeria shows resilience. China is watching Iran conflict as a strategic gift. India's PSU bank stocks in focus after Q4 results.

key_themes: Iran war and its macroeconomic impact (oil, inflation, supply chains)
AI-driven market bifurcation (winners vs losers in tech)
Central bank divergence (Fed delayed cuts, ECB torn)
Reshoring and semiconductor supply chain realignment
Consumer strain from high gas prices and mortgage rates
Geopolitical risk premium in energy and defense
Labor market resilience despite geopolitical shocks

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Iran war escalation and oil supply disruption
risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy stocks (XOM, CVX) and commodities; hedge with gold or oil futures; reduce exposure to airlines and consumer discretionary.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI-driven market shock and tech bubble risk
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify within tech; favor companies with strong fundamentals and AI monetization (NVDA, TSM, MSFT); avoid overvalued software names without clear AI strategy.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central bank policy misstep (Fed too late, ECB too early)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain duration-neutral bond positioning; favor floating rate instruments; hold cash for opportunities.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer strain from high gas prices and mortgage rates
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary; favor defensive sectors (healthcare, utilities); consider energy efficiency plays.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Geopolitical risk in emerging markets (Africa, China, Middle East)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Limit EM exposure to countries with strong fundamentals (India, Nigeria); avoid regions directly impacted by war or power constraints.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Regulatory and political uncertainty (FDA firing, marijuana rescheduling, DOJ probes)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Monitor regulatory developments; avoid speculative positions in heavily regulated sectors until clarity emerges.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy semiconductor stocks with strong AI exposure (NVDA, TSM, AMD on dips)
opportunities.rationale: Nvidia heading for record, Taiwan Semi has more business than it can handle, Apple-Intel deal signals long-term demand. AI expansion is secular trend.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate select big tech on weakness (Meta, IBM, Salesforce on pullbacks)
opportunities.rationale: Cramer bullish on Meta and IBM; Goldman's troubling trend may create buying opportunities for quality names. Meta's AI investments are paying off.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in energy infrastructure and oil producers (XOM, CVX, OIH)
opportunities.rationale: Iran war draining oil buffer, risk of $5 gasoline. Energy sector benefits from supply constraints and elevated prices. DOJ probe may cause short-term volatility but fundamentals strong.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy gold and silver as hedges (GLD, SLV, AGNCO)
opportunities.rationale: Precious metals headed for weekly gains; geopolitical uncertainty supports safe-haven demand. Cramer not bullish on gold but diversification warrants allocation.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider IPO participation in Quantinuum and Safepoint
opportunities.rationale: Quantinuum (quantum computing) and Safepoint (insurance) represent high-growth niches. Quantum computing is emerging, and Florida/Louisiana insurance market has pricing power.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Arista Networks on Cramer's recommendation
opportunities.rationale: Cramer says 'time to buy' Arista; strong networking demand from AI and cloud buildouts.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to airlines and consumer discretionary
defensive_moves.rationale: High fuel costs, geopolitical disruption, and consumer strain from gas prices. Spirit folding and Air India cutting flights are warning signs.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation or short-term Treasuries
defensive_moves.rationale: Apollo CEO warns of 35% chance of major market shock; Fed cuts delayed. Cash provides optionality to buy dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with VIX calls or put spreads on S&P 500
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risk (Iran) and AI-driven volatility could trigger sharp selloffs. Low-cost hedges protect against tail risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Rotate into healthcare and utilities for defensiveness
defensive_moves.rationale: Thermo Fisher and Becton Dickinson show strong execution; healthcare is defensive. Utilities benefit from stable demand regardless of macro.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid software stocks until sentiment improves
defensive_moves.rationale: Cramer notes market hates software; Cloudflare layoffs and Goldman's troubling trend suggest headwinds. Wait for better entry points.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets likely to remain volatile but supported by strong earnings and jobs data. Iran war developments will be key catalyst. S&P 500 could test new highs if ceasefire holds, but risk of escalation could trigger 5-10% correction. Energy and defense stocks will outperform. Tech may see rotation from software to semis.
market_outlook.long_term: AI-driven productivity gains and reshoring (Apple-Intel deal) support structural growth. However, persistent inflation, delayed Fed cuts, and geopolitical fragmentation (Iran, China) create headwinds. Expect higher volatility regime. Diversification across sectors and geographies is critical. Emerging markets with strong fundamentals (India, Nigeria) offer long-term value.
key_catalysts: Iran-US ceasefire negotiations outcome (next few days/weeks)
Trump-Xi summit (geopolitical implications for trade and Taiwan)
Fed meeting minutes and guidance on rate cuts
Nvidia earnings (next month) as AI bellwether
Oil inventory data and strategic petroleum reserve releases
US CPI and PCE inflation data (May/June)
Quantinuum and Safepoint IPO pricing and demand
China's stimulus measures for local government debt
watch_list: Iran war: Hormuz Strait shipping, oil prices, ceasefire talks
Fed: Rate cut expectations, inflation data, Beige Book
AI: Nvidia stock price, Cloudflare layoffs impact, Goldman's big tech trend
Energy: WTI and Brent crude, gasoline prices, DOJ probe outcomes
Currencies: USD/JPY, EUR/USD, NZD/USD (brain drain impact)
Emerging markets: Kenya data centre decision, Nigeria stock market, China energy imports
Crypto: Bitcoin and Ethereum price action, regulatory developments
IPOs: Quantinuum, Safepoint, and other upcoming listings
Consumer: Gas prices, mortgage rates, retail sales data
Healthcare: FDA leadership changes, hantavirus outbreak
